stories: UK fiscal shock: April borrowing prints £24.3bn (above expectations), putting the “rates + fiscal” mix back on the tape.
UK consumer warning: retail sales fall as petrol demand sees the biggest drop in six years.
Eurozone flash PMIs: composite drops in May, with services softness reviving recession/ECB downside risk.
US rates regime: markets keep pushing back on the “cuts” narrative—December hike odds are creeping into Fed funds pricing.
Oil regime remains unstable: OPEC+ “more barrels” talk collides with persistent geopolitical risk premium.
US–Iran escalation watch: blockade posture + reported briefings on potential operations keep tail-risk elevated.
Ukraine targets Russian oil infrastructure: Zelenskyy says May’s long-range plan is largely on track.
NVDA options anomaly: short-dated calls traded at a premium to puts into earnings (positive skew—rare for single stocks).
Insider signal (AMRZ): CEO Jan Philipp Jenisch keeps buying stock in size; additional officer buying adds to the cluster.
Wildcard (trade backdrop): updated map of where US–China tariff rates stand—useful for rapid margin/supply-chain stress tests.
